calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francisco Gutierrez for and with Lorraine Luna. John Boessenecker for and with (objector) Maria Clarke.

The Court confirmed that there were not objections to the creditors claim for funeral expenses, that there is an objection with respect to the request for statutory fees for the executor, that it has read the papers and asked if there was an unreasonable delay in closing resulting in expense to be surcharged. Mr. Boessenecker reported that Ms. Luna admits she agreed to waive fees, that Ms. Clarke relied on that and that she lost $15,000.00 in excess interest. The Court asked if the delay was unreasonable and that if it was not there is not a reason to surcharge. Mr. Boessenecker stated that it does not take 2 years to close an estate like this. Mr. Gutierrez stated that there is an issue regarding the trustees preventing Mrs. Clarke from refinancing, that the beneficiaries of the trustees cannot be located to be removed from the reconveyance, that prior to Mr. Boessenecker they had proposed a stipulation to Mrs. Clarke that financing would be arranged, that at this point they have a way to close the estate and that Ms. Luna has cooperated. Mr. Boessenecker clarified that he is not saying it was done in bad faith, that it just took too long and that the delays were not reasonable. Mr. Gutierrez stated that they had been provided a good faith estimate for a loan that Mrs. Clarke qualified for in October 2014. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Granted Per Written Order
